steps.title: Translate "at least 25 items"
steps.content:
With $x$ notebooks and $y$ pencil packs, the total number of items is $x + y$. "At least 25" means the total is greater than or equal to 25:
$$x + y \geq 25$$

steps.title: Translate "total cost not over 95 dollars"
steps.content:
Notebooks cost 2 dollars each and pencil packs cost 5 dollars each, so the total cost in dollars is $2x + 5y$. "Not over 95 dollars" means less than or equal to 95:
$$2x + 5y \leq 95$$

steps.title: Match against the choices
steps.content:
The system is $x + y \geq 25$ together with $2x + 5y \leq 95$.
  • $x + y \ge 25$ and $2x + 5y \le 95$: $x+y \ge 25$ and $2x+5y \le 95$ — matches both inequalities exactly. Correct.
  • $x + y \ge 25$ and $2x + 5y \ge 95$: $x+y \ge 25$ and $2x+5y \ge 95$ — reverses the cost inequality, allowing orders that exceed 95 dollars. Eliminated.
  • $x + 5y \le 25$ and $x + y \ge 95$: $x+5y \le 25$ and $x+y \ge 95$ — mixes up the coefficients and reverses both directions. Eliminated.
  • $x + y \le 25$ and $2x + 5y \ge 95$: $x+y \le 25$ and $2x+5y \ge 95$ — reverses both inequalities, so it caps the count and requires a minimum cost. Eliminated.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
